title: Planting Trees to Help Nature on an Island
summary: On the Isle of Man, a group called the Manx Wildlife Trust has planted 30,000 trees to create a special forest called a temperate rainforest. This forest is good for animals, plants, and people. Volunteers helped plant the trees at a place called Creg y Cowin, and they finished two years early. The trees will make the air and water cleaner, help stop floods, and make people feel happy and healthy. In the future, cows and sheep might live in the forest to help it grow naturally. The project is part of a bigger plan to plant more trees across the island and other places. It is paid for by a company called Aviva. The trees will take many years to grow big, but they will make the island a better home for everyone.

living things like cows and sheep

options: They make people feel happy and healthy
questions.correct_answer: They make people feel happy and healthy

questions.question: What animals might live in the forest in the future?
Who paid for the tree planting project?

background_read: The Isle of Man is a small island between Great Britain and Ireland. A temperate rainforest is a type of forest that is wet and cool, with lots of moss and lichens on trees. Planting trees helps nature by giving homes to animals, cleaning the air and water, and making places beautiful for people to enjoy. Volunteers are important because they work together to help the environment without asking for money.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Manx Wildlife Trust and volunteers
WHAT: Planted 30,000 trees to create a temperate rainforest
WHERE: At Creg y Cowin on the Isle of Man
WHY: To help nature, stop floods, clean water, and make people healthy

perspectives.description: Planting trees helps protect nature and animals.
